title: The Sword & Sorcery Saga - Part One - What is Sword & Sorcery?
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zzWIE6Sg_E0

Daniel (@dungeondive) kicks off his comprehensive Sword & Sorcery Saga series with an introduction to the genre and its key characteristics. He clarifies that while he's no expert, he's collected over 200 books to explore across sword & sorcery, sword & planet, and heroic fantasy subgenres. Rather than focusing heavily on Robert E. Howard's Conan, Daniel frames his series as a quest for knowledge, using respected anthologies edited by L. Sprague de Camp and Lin Carter as guides through the genre.

The video establishes defining elements that separate sword & sorcery from epic fantasy: stories are typically shorter with a narrower scope, focus on exciting scenarios over world-building, don't threaten the world's fate, and feature pragmatic heroes motivated by survival and plunder rather than altruism. Daniel also addresses the genre's problematic elements—misogyny, racism, and idealized cover art—with a commitment to discussing both the positive qualities and troubling aspects of these classic stories.

----

What aspects of sword & sorcery appeal to you most—the action and adventure, the character dynamics, or the worldbuilding approach?
